prior.content_md:
**Registration scope and portal mechanics**

All importers, exporters, producers, and quota-authorisation managers must register in the European Commission's F-Gas Portal (established under Implementing Regulation (EU) 2024/2473, the 'F-gas Portal Act'). Registration is a prerequisite for all customs procedures involving F-gas substances and equipment, with the sole exception of temporary storage. The portal manages the HFC quota phase-down, import/export licensing, and reporting obligations under Regulation (EU) 2024/573.

For the workspace's trade lanes (Americas→EU, Asia→EU air freight; intra-EU road), the most immediately relevant registration categories are: (a) **importer of pre-charged equipment** (refrigeration, AC, heat-pump equipment, or metered dose inhalers containing HFCs listed in Annex I, Section 1); (b) **bulk HFC importer** (for entities releasing bulk F-gases for free circulation); and (c) **exporter of F-gas-containing products/equipment**. Where the workspace acts as customs declarant agent rather than importer-of-record, it must verify that the client principal is registered in the correct category before goods arrive.

Registration requires: a physical EU address where human and technical resources are permanently present; only one importer/producer registration per physical address is permitted; beneficial-owner overlap between registrations results in quota entitlement for only one entity. For quota allocation eligibility, the applicant must demonstrate at least 3 years of experience in trading chemicals or servicing relevant F-gas equipment.

**Published requirements vs. still-developing guidance**

The core F-Gas Portal Act (Implementing Regulation (EU) 2024/2473) and registration guidelines were published by the European Commission on 17 March 2025. TARIC codes for F-gas-affected goods have been integrated by the Commission. The interconnection of the F-Gas Portal with the EU Single Window Environment for Customs came into force on 3 March 2025, though national single-window environments at Member-State level, including Germany, remain in rollout. The Commission is continuing to publish product-specific exemptions on a rolling basis (example: blood transport boxes and cryogenic freezers exempted 16 December 2024).

**Data fields the workspace must collect per shipment**

For each cargo movement involving F-gas-containing equipment or bulk F-gases, the workspace should collect and retain: (1) F-Gas Portal registration/licence number of the importer or exporter; (2) Annex classification of the substance(s) (Annex I, II, or III); (3) GWP value and CO₂-equivalent quantity of F-gases in the consignment; (4) quota authorisation reference (for HFC-containing equipment released for free circulation); (5) declaration of conformity reference for refillable containers (where applicable); (6) customs declaration fields as specified in Article 23 of Regulation (EU) 2024/573.

**Annual reporting obligations**

From 2025, importers of HFCs must report all quantities (no minimum threshold) to the Commission via the F-Gas Portal by 31 March each year for the previous calendar year. For other F-gases, the threshold remains at 100 t CO₂eq or 1 metric tonne. Quota-holders who placed no HFCs on the market must still submit a 'nil report'. Where HFC quantities reach ≥ 1,000 t CO₂eq, an independent auditor verification report is due by 30 April. The auditor must be separately registered in the F-Gas Portal.

*Source: EU Regulation Concerning Fluorinated Greenhouse Gases, Umweltbundesamt. https://www.umweltbundesamt.de/en/topics/climate-energy/fluorinated-greenhouse-gases-fully-halogenated-cfcs/statutes-regulations/eu-regulation-concerning-fluorinated-greenhouse; F-gas Portal, European Commission — DG CLIMA. https://climate.ec.europa.eu/eu-action/fluorinated-greenhouse-gases/f-gas-portal_en; F-gases in Equipment and Products — Stakeholder Obligations, European Commission. https://climate.ec.europa.eu/eu-action/fluorinated-greenhouse-gases/stakeholder-obligations/f-gases-equipment-and-products_en*
